title: microlaena acuminata
intro: Microlaena acuminata, also known as Poverty Grass, is a native Australian grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Microlaena acuminata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Microlaena acuminata include infusions and decoctions made from the leaves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
